arguments.task: Open official Gladly pages needed to verify developer/API availability, authentication methods, pricing or contact-sales gating, and any official MCP or integrations info. Prefer first-party pages on gladly.com. Read the page content and return: 1) one-line product description, 2) whether there is a public API/docs and what auth methods are documented, 3) pricing or sales-contact language relevant to access tier, 4) whether the API appears REST, GraphQL, both, or none, 5) any official MCP/server mention. Include exact canonical URLs actually visited and summarize the supporting text from each. Do not rely on search-result snippets or third-party sites.

input.thought: Use the browser tool to inspect first-party Gladly pages and extract official evidence.

raw_result.description: Gladly is a customer service platform that unifies customer data to help brands deliver personalized support.
raw_result.auth_notes: Gladly's developer docs describe Basic Authentication using an agent email address together with an API token in the form email:api_token.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public pricing was not available from the gathered first-party pages; the site routed pricing intent to contact/demo flows, indicating sales-gated access.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer documentation is available for a REST API with JSON payloads and documented authentication, but the gathered evidence did not enumerate the full endpoint surface in detail.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol mention was found on the gathered Gladly first-party pages, including the developer portal and integrations page.

raw_result.blocker: Sales-gated platform access/pricing means a first toolkit appears technically feasible from the public REST docs, but practical build access likely requires manual vendor engagement.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: No rate-limit details were verified from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer portal exists; REST API and authentication are documented publicly; pricing/access appears routed through contact-sales rather than self-serve signup.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The strongest gathered evidence supports REST API and Basic Auth with API token, but the exact structured access tier and buildability depend on interpreting contact-sales routing without a directly readable canonical pricing page or explicit API access entitlement statement.

result.description: Gladly is a customer service platform that unifies customer data to help brands deliver personalized support.
result.auth_notes: Gladly's developer docs describe Basic Authentication using an agent email address together with an API token in the form email:api_token.
result.gating_reason: Public pricing was not available from the gathered first-party pages; the site routed pricing intent to contact/demo flows, indicating sales-gated access.

api_surface.breadth: Public developer documentation is available for a REST API with JSON payloads and documented authentication, but the gathered evidence did not enumerate the full endpoint surface in detail.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol mention was found on the gathered Gladly first-party pages, including the developer portal and integrations page.
